title: Logs errors honors the --lines limit
description: Black-box smoke for `orca logs errors --lines <n>` with a preseeded errors log.
input: Seed an errors log with three lines, run `orca logs errors --lines 1`, and verify the output only includes the newest errors entry.
command:
bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_ROOT=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP_ROOT" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP_ROOT/home/.orca/logs"; cat > "$TMP_ROOT/home/.orca/logs/errors.log" <<EOF
[ERROR] one
[ERROR] two
[ERROR] three
EOF
OUTPUT=$(env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_ROOT/home" ORCA_HOME="$TMP_ROOT/home/.orca" node dist/bin/orca.js logs errors --lines 1); printf "%s\n" "$OUTPUT"; ! printf "%s" "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "\[ERROR\] one"; ! printf "%s" "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "\[ERROR\] two"; printf "logs-errors-lines-ok\n"'
